appShell.promptPlaceholder: Ask about a symptom, result, or trend
appShell.startDesktop: Start the desktop app to load local records.
jobs.title: Background work
jobs.description: Document analysis and research tasks stay visible while you move through your records.
jobs.emptyDescription: Document analysis and deep research will appear here.
jobs.cancelled: The task was replaced by newer work.
jobs.interrupted: This task was interrupted when the desktop app closed.
jobs.aiChatDescription: Working from your approved health context
developer.title: Developer
developer.description: Inspect local request timing, Codex commands, and failure details while you test the app.
developer.privacy: Prompt text, extracted results, and secrets are excluded. Only bounded metadata and errors are stored locally.
developer.interrupted: The app closed before this Codex call finished.
developer.calls.title: LLM calls
developer.calls.description: Each row represents a real Codex CLI invocation from this app.
developer.calls.emptyDescription: Start document analysis or an AI conversation to capture request details here.
developer.logs.title: Event log
developer.logs.description: Renderer stages and bounded error details, newest first.
developer.logs.emptyDescription: Document preparation and AI activity will appear here as you use the app.
desktop.requiredTail: The raw Vite URL is only an internal renderer for Tauri.
addResult.label: Add result
body.hero.label: Health attention summary
body.hero.inRangeDetail: Your tracked markers are in range. Keep logging results over time.
body.hero.noCurrentDetail: Latest results, recent symptoms, and current conditions show no follow-up status.
body.hero.emptyDetail: Add your first lab or vital to begin tracking your body.
body.hero.attentionDetail: Review these with your clinician rather than acting on them alone.
body.hero.monitorDetail: Track changes over time and discuss them at your next visit.
body.organs.title: Organs
body.organs.description: Select a body area to filter records.
body.anatomy.label: Interactive anatomy map
body.recent.title: Recent for this organ
body.recent.description: Latest labs and symptoms linked to this organ.
body.recent.noResults: No results linked to this organ yet.
body.dailyLog.description: Whole-body activity, smoking, alcohol, and notes. These entries are not linked to the selected organ.
body.summary.empty: {organ} has no linked records yet. Add results, symptoms, or conditions to build a local history.
body.summary.normal: No current follow-up signal is linked to {organ}. Keep tracking changes over time.
body.summary.monitor: Current {organ} records include follow-up items to monitor. Use this as a discussion aid, not a diagnosis.
body.summary.attention: Current {organ} records include a discuss-soon follow-up. Use this as a discussion aid, not a diagnosis.
conditions.title: Conditions
conditions.empty: No conditions tracked for this area.
conditions.placeholder.name: Hypothyroidism
history.results.title: Result trends
history.results.description: Scan lab markers and vitals by follow-up priority, organ, and latest movement.
history.symptoms.title: Symptom history
history.symptoms.description: Review symptoms by severity, organ, and when they occurred.
history.summary.clearToSee: Try clearing the filter or search to see your records.
history.summary.addFirstResult: Add your first lab or vital to start a timeline.
history.summary.resultsInRange: Your tracked results are in the normal range. Keep logging over time.
history.summary.logSymptom: Log a symptom to track patterns over time.
history.summary.severeDetail: These are worth raising with your clinician at the next opportunity.
history.summary.symptomsWatchDetail: Mild to moderate symptoms worth tracking as patterns over time.
history.summary.mildDetail: Nothing severe logged. Keep noting changes as they happen.
history.empty.clearResults: Clear the filter or search to see your records.
history.empty.addResult: Add a lab or vital to start your timeline.
intake.document.emptyDescription: Add a result, choose a file, or type a result in the prompt bar.
intake.document.noResults: No results to review yet. Add a result below to continue.
intake.symptom.name: Symptom
intake.activity.placeholder: Slept 7h, walked 30 min, mild headache after lunch.
intake.activity.description: This saves a local daily entry, not a diagnosis.
intake.description.activity: Save structured activity, exposure, and notes for today.
intake.description.document: Review the extracted results, edit every field as needed, then accept.
medications.emptyTitle: No medications or supplements saved yet.
medications.emptyDescription: Add regular items so dose changes stay beside your labs and symptoms.
medications.draftDescription: Review the prompt draft before saving.
medications.defaultDescription: Track dose, timing, and why you take it.
medications.draftReady: Prompt draft ready. Edit any field, then save.
medications.name: Name
medications.placeholder.name: Vitamin D
medications.placeholder.notes: Instructions, brand, reactions, or clinician notes
medications.savedLocal: Saved to the encrypted local database on this device.
settings.profile.title: Profile
settings.ai.title: AI Settings
settings.ai.thinkingEffortDescription: Controls how long Codex thinks before responding.
settings.ai.codexModelsUnavailable: Using bundled Codex defaults until the CLI model catalog is available.
settings.ai.allowRemoteDescription: Keep off unless you explicitly want health context sent to the selected remote provider.
settings.ai.reasoningEffortRequired: Choose a thinking effort before saving.
settings.ai.baseUrlRequired: Enter a base URL before saving.
settings.ai.apiKeyEnvVarRequired: Enter the API key environment variable before saving.
settings.export.title: Data Export
settings.export.description: Save a SQLCipher copy of the unlocked local database.
settings.export.passphraseTooShort: Enter at least 12 characters after leading and trailing spaces are removed.
settings.export.ready: Passphrases match. The encrypted copy is ready to export.
gate.body.setup: Choose a passphrase before the app stores health records on this device.
gate.body.migration: We found an older local database. Encrypt it before opening the dashboard.
gate.body.locked: Enter your passphrase to load the health records stored on this device.
gate.help.setup: Use at least 12 characters. Keep it somewhere safe; this app cannot recover it.
gate.help.locked: Use the passphrase you chose for this local database.
gate.passphraseTooShort: Enter at least 12 characters after leading and trailing spaces are removed.
chat.none: No conversations yet. Ask a question to start a saved thread.
chat.description: Responses are advisory, saved locally, and not emergency guidance.
chat.placeholder: Example: Summarize my thyroid markers and questions to discuss with a clinician.
chat.emptyBody: Ask about a lab trend, symptom pattern, or note. Remote context requires Settings approval.
documents.title: Documents
documents.description: Extract results from PDFs and images with your configured AI, or save a local Apple Health export summary.
documents.pdfDescription: Drop a PDF or image. Codex extracts draft results for you to review and accept.
documents.appleDescription: Choose the unzipped export.xml file. This saves counts and date range only, not metrics.
documents.emptyAppleTitle: No Apple Health exports imported yet.
documents.emptyAppleDescription: Import an export.xml file to keep a local summary beside your records.
lifestyle.title: Lifestyle guidance
lifestyle.description: Local suggestions from your saved records. Refine with AI for a tailored plan.
lifestyle.generated.routine.cautiousMove: Keep movement light and write down symptom changes before changing intensity.
lifestyle.generated.routine.dailyMovemen: Keep one repeatable daily movement block on the calendar.
lifestyle.generated.routine.dailyLog: Log activity, symptoms, cigarettes, drinks, and notes once per day.
lifestyle.generated.routine.reviewRegime: Review active medications and supplements against new results each week.
lifestyle.generated.routine.addRegimen: Add medications or supplements you take regularly before comparing patterns.
lifestyle.generated.routine.deepResearch: Use Deep Research for a structured review before your next clinician conversation.
lifestyle.generated.breathing.bodyCautio: Keep this gentle: slow nasal breathing can help track stress or breath-related symptoms without changing medications or treatment.
lifestyle.generated.breathing.bodyDaily: A short breathing block gives you a repeatable baseline to compare against sleep, symptoms, and activity notes.
lifestyle.generated.breathing.action: Try 5 minutes at a comfortable pace, then log how you feel.
lifestyle.generated.exercise.bodyCautiou: Use low-intensity walking or mobility until the flagged results or severe symptoms are reviewed.
lifestyle.generated.exercise.bodyNormal: Consistent easy movement is easier to compare with marker trends than occasional hard sessions.
lifestyle.generated.exercise.actionCauti: Use 10-15 easy minutes and stop if symptoms worsen.
lifestyle.generated.exercise.actionNorma: Aim for a comfortable 20-minute walk or mobility block.
lifestyle.generated.activity.bodyExposur: Keep cigarettes, drinks, activity, and symptom notes in the same log so patterns are visible before making changes.
lifestyle.generated.activity.bodyRepeat: Reuse the activities that leave useful notes and compare them with symptoms and results over time.
lifestyle.generated.activity.bodyStart: A small daily log makes lifestyle advice specific instead of generic.
lifestyle.generated.activity.action: Log activity, cigarettes, drinks, and one short note today.
lifestyle.generated.review.bodyRegimen: Keep active medications and supplements beside new results so dose, timing, symptoms, and labs are reviewed together.
lifestyle.generated.review.bodyHealth: Review markers, symptoms, and daily logs once per week before adding more complicated routines.
lifestyle.generated.review.action: Set one weekly review block and write clinician-discussion questions.
research.advisory: AI output is advisory and saved to Chat. Use it to prepare questions, not as diagnosis or emergency guidance.
research.reviewPrompt: Review the full prompt before sending
research.setupRequired: Configure Codex CLI and allow remote AI health context in Settings before starting research.
research.reviewRequired: Open and review the full prompt before starting research.
research.promptReviewed: Prompt reviewed. Research is ready to start.
lifestyle.refineUnavailable: Configure Codex CLI and allow remote AI health context in Settings to refine this plan.
markdown.label: Markdown response
toast.waitForAi: Wait for the current AI response before sending another prompt.
toast.usingAttachedFile: Using the attached file for result intake.
toast.draftedResult: Drafted a result. Review before saving.
toast.draftedMedication: Drafted a medication. Review before saving.
toast.providerNotConnected: Provider is not connected to live chat yet.
toast.aiSaved: AI response saved to conversation history.
toast.aiSettingsSession: AI settings kept for this session.
toast.refreshFailed: The change was saved, but the latest dashboard could not be refreshed.
toast.newDatabaseSelected: New database selected. Choose a passphrase to create it.
toast.databaseSelected: Database selected. Enter its passphrase to open it.
toast.fileTooLarge: File is too large to analyze or import.
toast.extractManual: Couldn't extract results. Enter them manually.
toast.documentAnalysisFailed: Document analysis failed. Enter the results manually.
toast.fillResultFields: Fill in marker, value, and date for at least one result.
ai.providerNotLive: {provider} is not available for live chat.
ai.remoteContextBlocked: Remote health context is disabled. Enable it in Settings before sending health details to this provider.
document.choosePdfImage: Choose a PDF or image result file.
document.noLlmEnabled: No LLM is enabled for document analysis. In Settings, choose Codex CLI, select a model, and allow remote AI health context.
document.extractError: Couldn't extract results from this file. Enter them manually.
document.renderFailed: The document could not be prepared for AI extraction.
appleHealth.chooseExport: Choose the unzipped Apple Health export.xml file.
appleHealth.tooLarge: Apple Health export is too large for browser import. Use a smaller export summary.
appleHealth.noRecords: No Apple Health records found in export.xml.
userState.apiKeyEnvVar: API key env var must be an environment variable name like OPENAI_API_KEY.
runtime.tauriOnly: This app runs only in the Tauri desktop app because health records are stored in local encrypted SQLite.

history.result.editDescription: Correct the value, date, follow-up priority, or organ. Trends update after saving.

intake.organDescription: Confirm the organ or body system before saving.

settings.ai.notLiveWarning: This provider is not available for live chat.
chat.setupPlaceholder: Configure an LLM and allow health context in Settings before sending.
chat.setupRequired: Choose an LLM and model. Remote providers also need health-context access.
documents.emptyReportsDescription: Accepted document imports appear here so you can unlink or remove them later.
documents.deleteReportConfirm: Delete this report record but keep linked results?
documents.deleteReportResultsConfirm: Delete this report and hide its linked results?
toast.providerPlanned: {provider} is not available for live chat.
toast.regimenDeleted: Regimen item deleted from active views.
toast.reportDeletedWithResults: Report and linked results deleted from active views.
ai.notConfigured: AI is not configured yet. Choose an LLM in Settings, select a model, and allow remote health context when needed.
charts.lab.title: Lab trend
charts.lab.description: Review one marker over time against its reference range.
charts.lab.noNumeric: No numeric lab results to chart.
charts.lab.noNumericDescription: Save numeric lab values to see trends over time.
charts.organ.title: Organ trend
charts.organ.firstReading: First numeric reading for this marker.
charts.symptoms.title: Symptom severity over time
charts.symptoms.description: Weekly view of tracked symptom severity.
charts.symptoms.empty: Log symptoms to see severity over time.
charts.symptoms.emptyDescription: Weekly grouping keeps repeated symptoms scannable.
charts.regimen.title: Regimen timeline
charts.regimen.description: Medication and supplement periods based on saved start and stop dates.
charts.regimen.emptyDescription: Add medications or supplements with dates to see periods here.
charts.documents.coverageDescription: Date ranges covered by imported files. Lab documents show import dates when report dates are not available.
charts.documents.emptyDescription: Import Apple Health XML or lab documents to see coverage.
charts.coverage.title: Context coverage
charts.coverage.description: Records included in this prompt context.
charts.matrix.title: Follow-up priority matrix
charts.matrix.description: Scan saved follow-up priorities across recent result dates. Range position is available in each result's details.
history.summary.followUpDetail: Based on the follow-up priority saved with each marker's latest result.
